entry.title: Re: Deleted SLP contact
entry.content:
If I understand correctly you want to recreate the contact in SLP, but a (deactivated) contact with the same e-mail address already exists. SLP will not allow you to create a new contact with the exact same e-mail address of a contact that is either active or deactivated.

If you are unable to reactivate the existing contact (I assume you tried this), then what you can try is the following:

1. Export the contact using SM Admin

2. Change the e-mail address of the existing contact record by adding something to it (for example "delete.<email address>

3. Reload the contact record for this specific contact.

Now that the e-mail address of the deactivated contact has been changed you should be able to add a new contact to to the supplier in SLP with the original e-mail address.

entry.title: Re: s_alr_87013611 header is not displayed
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question on SAP Community’s blog.

Yes — this is a known issue that can happen in transaction S_ALR_87013611 after certain SAP support package updates or system upgrades. The disappearance of the report header (e.g., cost center group or report description at the top) is often due to changes in the way Report Painter/Report Writer layouts or form variants are handled.

Root Causes (Most Common)

1. Missing or reset Report Header Form

After the update:

  • The report may have lost its assigned form (used to display the header and layout info).
  • The form might have been reset, overwritten, or deleted.

 

2. Report Painter Form not properly migrated

If you upgraded from an older system or applied new SAP Notes, sometimes the Report Painter forms (used for the layout and headers) are not re-generated or are incompatible with newer versions.

 

3. User-specific or variant-specific layout missing

If users were relying on a saved layout or user-specific variant, and those were deleted or reset, the header might disappear from view.

 

I hope this will give you some seeds to understand the issue.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: s_alr_87013611 header is not displayed
entry.content:
Dear user,

Here is how you can proceed :

STEP 1: Identify the Report Group

  1. Go to transaction S_ALR_87013611.
  2. On the selection screen, note the Report name and Report Group (e.g., 1VK_001 and 1VK).
  3. Open transaction GR55.
  4. Enter the Report Group (e.g., 1VK) and execute.
  5. Find the report you're using (e.g., 1VK_001), select it, and click Display.

STEP 2: Check the Assigned Form

  1. In the report display, note the Form name.
  2. Open transaction GR32 (Change Form).
  3. Enter the form name you found, and click "Display" or "Change".

If no form is assigned, or the form is blank, that’s the reason why the header is missing.

STEP 3: Reassign or Create a Form with Header

Option A: Reassign an existing form with a header

  1. Use GR32 or GR33 to find standard forms (e.g., 1VK_FORM01, 1VK_FORM02, etc.).
  2. In transaction GRR2 (Change Report), assign the form to your report.

Option B: Create a new form with header

  1. Go to GR31 (Create Form).
  2. Use an existing form as a reference.
  3. In the form layout:
    • Add header rows (Report title, Cost Center, Fiscal Year, etc.).
    • Save and assign this form to your report via GRR2.

STEP 4: Test the Report Again

  1. Go back to S_ALR_87013611.
  2. Run the report.
  3. The header should now appear, showing the cost center and other key information.

Let me know if you need more details!

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: Sending ERP initiated service entry sheets to Ariba B...
entry.content:
In case anyone runs into the same issue, here's what caused the problem:

In our deployment the numbering of the PO line items in Ariba (1, 2, 3, etc) does not correspond with the numbering of the CC PO line items in ECC (10, 20, 30, etc). 

The add-on program responsible for sending the ERP initiated SeS back to Ariba Buying does not take this into account, and uses the line numbering of the CC PO in ECC (10, 20 , 30, etc) as the PO line item reference in the SeS message.

Ariba Buying does not generate an error and the SeS is accepted by the Buying system.

The Business Network (BN) however does not accept the SeS. But as no error message is generated this was impossible for us as a customer to see. By analysing the log files, Ariba Support determined that the BN does not accept the SES line if the PO line reference in the SeS (10, 20, 30 etc) does noet match a PO line number on the BN (1, 2, 3, etc).

This also explains why in some rare cases we did see a SeS appear on the BN: if the PO contains 10 or more line items and you happen to create a SeS in ECC for (as an example) PO line item 10 (which is line 1 in the Ariba PO). In that case the BN simply assumes that the SeS was intended for the tenth line item in the PO.

We resolved this by changing the standard add-on program that sends the ERP generated SeS back to Ariba Buying: it determines the corresponding Ariba PO line number (1, 2, 3, etc) and sends that back to Ariba Buying instead of the ECC CC PO line number (10, 20, 30, etc).

entry.title: Conquered the C_TS462_2021 Exam After Overcoming All the Challenges!
entry.content:
After facing countless hurdles and endless late-night study sessions, I finally passed the C_TS462_2021 exam! It wasn’t easy, but with the right resources and some smart strategies, I made it through. A huge shoutout to passexam4sure for providing the perfect materials that helped me stay focused and prepared.

The C_TS462_2021 exam is for those who are looking to earn the SAP Certified Application Associate – SAP S/4HANA Sales 2021 certification. It tests your knowledge in areas like sales order processing, billing, master data management, and the integration of sales processes with other SAP solutions.

If you're feeling stuck or overwhelmed, just know that persistence and the right tools can make all the difference. The road may be tough, but trust me, it’s totally worth it when you reach the finish line. Keep pushing forward, and success will follow!

entry.title: TransactionNotification
entry.content:
I have implemented a validation in SAP Business One using SBO_SP_TransactionNotification to prevent issuing more materials than the planned quantity. However, I'm facing an issue:

If I issue material (e.g., 10 units), receive part of it in production (e.g., 8 units), and return the unused quantity (e.g., 1 unit), then try to re-issue the returned material again (e.g., 1 unit), the system still blocks the transaction, even though the return should allow re-issuance up to the planned quantity.

How can I correctly modify my TransactionNotification logic to:

Allow reissuing of returned materials without exceeding the originally planned quantity?

Ensure that net issued quantity = (total issues - total returns + current issue) is validated against the planned quantity for the same item?

IF :transaction_type IN ('A', 'U') AND :object_type = '60' THEN

DECLARE docCount INT;

SELECT COUNT(*) INTO docCount
FROM (
SELECT
T0."BaseEntry",
T0."ItemCode",

-- Sum of current issue quantity
SUM(T0."Quantity") AS CurrentIssueQty,

-- Planned quantity from Production Order
(
SELECT SUM(W1."PlannedQty")
FROM "WOR1" W1
WHERE W1."DocEntry" = T0."BaseEntry" AND W1."ItemCode" = T0."ItemCode"
) AS PlannedQty

FROM "IGE1" T0
WHERE T0."DocEntry" = :list_of_cols_val_tab_del
GROUP BY T0."BaseEntry", T0."ItemCode"

HAVING
(

(
IFNULL((
SELECT SUM(I1."Quantity")
FROM "IGE1" I1
INNER JOIN "OIGN" I2 ON I1."DocEntry" = I2."DocEntry"
WHERE I1."BaseEntry" = T0."BaseEntry"
AND I1."BaseType" = 202
AND I1."ItemCode" = T0."ItemCode"
AND I1."DocEntry" <> :list_of_cols_val_tab_del
), 0)
+
SUM(T0."Quantity")
-
IFNULL((
SELECT SUM(R1."Quantity")
FROM "IGN1" R1
INNER JOIN "OIGN" R2 ON R1."DocEntry" = R2."DocEntry"
WHERE R1."BaseEntry" = T0."BaseEntry"
AND R1."BaseType" = 60
AND R1."ItemCode" = T0."ItemCode"
), 0)
)
>
(
SELECT SUM(W1."PlannedQty")
FROM "WOR1" W1
WHERE W1."DocEntry" = T0."BaseEntry" AND W1."ItemCode" = T0."ItemCode"
)
)

) AS SubQuery;

IF :docCount > 0 THEN
error := 555;
error_message := 'Issuing quantity exceeds planned quantity (returns considered).';
END IF;

END IF;

 

 


----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
IF :transaction_type IN ('A', 'U') AND :object_type = '59' THEN

DECLARE docCount INT;

SELECT COUNT(*) INTO docCount
FROM (
SELECT
T0."BaseEntry",
SUM(T0."Quantity") AS CurrentReceiptQty,


IFNULL((
SELECT SUM(R1."Quantity")
FROM "IGN1" R1
INNER JOIN "OIGN" R2 ON R1."DocEntry" = R2."DocEntry"
WHERE R1."BaseEntry" = T0."BaseEntry"
AND R1."BaseType" = 202
AND R1."DocEntry" <> :list_of_cols_val_tab_del
), 0) AS PreviousReceiptQty,


(
IFNULL((
SELECT SUM(I1."Quantity")
FROM "IGE1" I1
INNER JOIN "OIGN" I2 ON I1."DocEntry" = I2."DocEntry"
WHERE I1."BaseEntry" = T0."BaseEntry"
AND I1."BaseType" = 202
), 0)
-
IFNULL((
SELECT SUM(RT1."Quantity")
FROM "IGN1" RT1
INNER JOIN "OIGN" RT2 ON RT1."DocEntry" = RT2."DocEntry"
WHERE RT1."BaseEntry" = T0."BaseEntry"
AND RT1."BaseType" = 60
), 0)
) AS NetIssuedQty,


(SELECT MAX("PlannedQty") FROM "OWOR" WHERE "DocEntry" = T0."BaseEntry") AS PlannedQty,


(SELECT SUM("PlannedQty") FROM "WOR1" WHERE "DocEntry" = T0."BaseEntry") AS TotalPlannedComponentQty

FROM "IGN1" T0
WHERE T0."DocEntry" = :list_of_cols_val_tab_del
GROUP BY T0."BaseEntry"

HAVING

(
(
IFNULL((
SELECT SUM(I1."Quantity")
FROM "IGE1" I1
INNER JOIN "OIGN" I2 ON I1."DocEntry" = I2."DocEntry"
WHERE I1."BaseEntry" = T0."BaseEntry"
AND I1."BaseType" = 202
), 0)
-
IFNULL((
SELECT SUM(RT1."Quantity")
FROM "IGN1" RT1
INNER JOIN "OIGN" RT2 ON RT1."DocEntry" = RT2."DocEntry"
WHERE RT1."BaseEntry" = T0."BaseEntry"
AND RT1."BaseType" = 60
), 0)
) = 0
)

OR


(
SUM(T0."Quantity") + IFNULL((
SELECT SUM(R1."Quantity")
FROM "IGN1" R1
INNER JOIN "OIGN" R2 ON R1."DocEntry" = R2."DocEntry"
WHERE R1."BaseEntry" = T0."BaseEntry"
AND R1."BaseType" = 202
AND R1."DocEntry" <> :list_of_cols_val_tab_del
), 0)
) >
(SELECT MAX("PlannedQty") FROM "OWOR" WHERE "DocEntry" = T0."BaseEntry") * (
(

IFNULL((
SELECT SUM(I1."Quantity")
FROM "IGE1" I1
INNER JOIN "OIGN" I2 ON I1."DocEntry" = I2."DocEntry"
WHERE I1."BaseEntry" = T0."BaseEntry"
AND I1."BaseType" = 202
), 0)
-
IFNULL((
SELECT SUM(RT1."Quantity")
FROM "IGN1" RT1
INNER JOIN "OIGN" RT2 ON RT1."DocEntry" = RT2."DocEntry"
WHERE RT1."BaseEntry" = T0."BaseEntry"
AND RT1."BaseType" = 60
), 0)
)
/
IFNULL((
SELECT SUM("PlannedQty") FROM "WOR1" WHERE "DocEntry" = T0."BaseEntry"
), 1)
)

) SubQuery;

IF :docCount > 0 THEN
error := 600;
error_message := 'You can only receive production output as per net issued material (after returns).';
END IF;

END IF;

entry.title: Re: CU50 in internal tables
entry.content:
thanks Sandra_Rossi for you feedback.
But I am still blocked. I can't have the same result than CU50. I have to much lines compare to CU50 result. 

I try to debug CS_BOM_EXPL_MAT_V2 during CU50 and copy the input in my program function.

-> I have to much line and I don't know how to retrieve my costs based on my costing variant.

Thanks  a lot for your help :
DATA: l_handle   TYPE bapicu-cfg_handle,
      l_instance TYPE bapicu-instance.

DATA : it_characts_vals     TYPE TABLE OF bapicuchv1,
       l_characts_vals      TYPE bapicuchv1,
       it_characts_vals_err TYPE TABLE OF bapicuchv.

DATA : ls_root_object  TYPE cuib_business_object,
       lv_guid         TYPE guid_32,
       ev_new_instance TYPE cuib_cuobj.

DATA : lt_stpox TYPE TABLE OF stpox,
       ls_stpox TYPE stpox.

" Étape 1 : Créer la config mémoire
CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_CFG_CREATE'
  EXPORTING
    object_id     = 'MATERIAL'
    root_type     = 'MARA'
    root_name     = 'MATERIAL'
    plant         = 'PLNT'
  IMPORTING
    cfg_handle    = l_handle
    root_instance = l_instance
  EXCEPTIONS
    error         = 1
    OTHERS        = 2.

" Étape 2 : Définir les valeurs de caractéristiques
l_characts_vals-atnam = 'CHARCT'.
l_characts_vals-atwtb = 'VALUE_CHARCT'.

APPEND l_characts_vals TO it_characts_vals.

CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_CFGINST_CHARCS_VALS_SET'
  EXPORTING
    object_id         = 'MATERIAL'
    instance          = l_instance
  TABLES
    characts_vals     = it_characts_vals
    characts_vals_err = it_characts_vals_err.

" Étape 3 : Associer à un objet logique et générer clé si besoin
ls_root_object-object_type = 'VBPS'.

SELECT SINGLE objkey FROM ibinown INTO ls_root_object-object_key
  WHERE instance = l_handle.  " Handle d'instance à partir de la config

IF sy-subrc <> 0.
  CALL FUNCTION 'GUID_CREATE'
    IMPORTING
      ev_guid_32 = lv_guid.

  CONCATENATE lv_guid 'MATERIAL' INTO ls_root_object-object_key SEPARATED BY '_'.
ENDIF.

CALL FUNCTION 'CE_C_SET_CBASE'.

" Étape 4 : Persister la config et obtenir le CUOBJ
CALL FUNCTION 'CUCB_CONFIGURATION_TO_DB'
  EXPORTING
    root_instance                 = l_handle
    root_object                   = ls_root_object
  IMPORTING
    new_instance                  = ev_new_instance
  EXCEPTIONS
    invalid_instance              = 1
    invalid_root_instance         = 2
    no_changes                    = 3
    already_registered_for_update = 4
    instance_is_a_classification  = 5
    OTHERS                        = 6.


  COMMIT WORK AND WAIT.

  " Étape 5 : Exploser nomenclature avec le nouveau CUOBJ
  CALL FUNCTION 'CS_BOM_EXPL_MAT_V2'
    EXPORTING
      aumgb = 'X'
      auskz = 'X'
      capid = 'PC01'
      ehndl = '1'
      emeng = 1
      datuv = sy-datum
      mtnrv = 'MATERIAL'
      werks = 'PLNT'
      knfba = 'X'
      cuobj = ev_new_instance
      ksbvo = 'X'
      mbwls = ' '
      mktls = ' '
      mdmps = 'X'
      mdnot = 'X'
      mehrs = 'X'
      rndkz = '1'
      norvl = 'X'
    TABLES
      stb   = lt_stpox.

Thanks for your help

SUBSTRING function could be the best way.

DATA(lv_ftype) = substring( val = lv_string off = ( strlen( lv_string ) - 3 ) len = 3 ). "to get "xls" (last 3 characters)

entry.title: Re: Delete Business Partner in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud is not possible
entry.content: If you can see it in the View Browser, you should be able to Create a Custom CDS View and then use that view in the Data Browser. You could also expose the custom cds view as a odata service api and call it from a tool like Postman.

entry.title: Re: what is the default role and grouping considered duri...
entry.content:
Regarding Business partner grouping determination in DEBMAS IDOC, there are a few dependencies (if customer number and account group are present in the IDOC or not, if the BP number is already taken in the system, if the default BP grouping is configured etc.). Please refer to section "Business Partner Grouping Determination Logic" of SAP Note 2479954 - DEBMAS/CREMAS IDocs in SAP S/4HANA Business Partner. It contains detailed description and should answer your question. 

Best regards,

Stanisław Stefanowski

entry.title: EML : Business Object Interface Exception Handling
entry.content:
Dear All

Please support that how to read messages returned by Business Object Interface programmatically.

We can read the response through debugging, but how to read same using ABAP.

 In below code

REPORTED DATA(reported)

OR

is having %MSG having the error message but final structure is only available at runtime.

MODIFY ENTITY I_ProductionOrderTP
CREATE FIELDS ( 
 product
 productionplant
 productionversion
 productionordertype
 orderplannedtotalqty
 productionunit
 basicschedulingtype
 )
AUTO FILL CID WITH VALUE #(
 (
 %data-product = 'FG228'
 %data-productionplant = '1010'
 %data-productionversion = '0001'
 %data-productionordertype = 'YBM1'
 %data-orderplannedtotalqty = 5
 %data-productionunit = 'ST'
 %data-basicschedulingtype = '4'
 )
 )
FAILED DATA(failed)
REPORTED DATA(reported)
MAPPED DATA(mapped).

IF failed IS INITIAL.
 COMMIT ENTITIES BEGIN
 RESPONSES
 FAILED DATA(failed_commit)
 REPORTED DATA(reported_commit).

 IF sy-subrc = 0 AND failed_commit IS INITIAL.
 CONVERT KEY OF i_productionordertp
 FROM TEMPORARY VALUE #( %pid = mapped-productionorder[ 1 ]-%pid
 %tmp = mapped-productionorder[ 1 ]-%key )
 TO FINAL(ls_finalkey).
 ENDIF.
 COMMIT ENTITIES END.

 " Number of created Order: ls_finalkey-productionorder

ELSE.
 ROLLBACK ENTITIES.
ENDIF.

  2. We want to create Jira incidents from actions directly within an alert manually

While setting up a subscription "Cloud ALM to Jira" we realized that the mapping template for JIRA Incidents in "Mapping Management" is missing. 
https://support.sap.com/en/alm/sap-cloud-alm/operations/expert-portal/intelligent-event-processing/integration-ticket-systems.html?anchorId=section_26365029

  • Event Situation to ServiceNow Incident (Vancouv / Wash. DC / Xanadu)

SAP Cloud ALM 

Best regards

Thomas

@satishbabu_bodepudi thank you for such a wonderful document.

I was replicating a similar scenario and faced some interesting issues which I wanted to share.

In my case, the outbound idoc triggered successfully upon creation of the PO and another inbound idoc is created as well. But the inbound idoc is in 51 status with error 'Fill out all required entry fields" (message no.00055). below is the screenshot.

I've debugged the IDOC process code FM IDOC_INPUT_ORDERS and understood that the field RV45A-KETDAT (requested delivery date) is not populated in the BDC data. The code is trying to fetch this from WLDAT which is blank. 

Surprisingly the value WLDAT in this FM comes from below piece of code.

However, this works only if the enhancement FSH_SPECIFIC_VEDA_ENH is active, which usually is not the case.

Then i felt may be im looking it too closer, and took a step back, and tried manually created a sales order. Then I observed that the 'Requested Delivery Date' is not something system proposes automatically. Then I checked the order type configuration (T.code VoV8), and tweaked below options. And then everything fell into place.

hoping this will save a lot of time for someone else atleast 🙂

entry.title: Re: No JSON payload template for event situations to crea...
entry.content:
The SAP Cloud ALM External API Management built-in Mappings are not intended to be an exhaustive list of all possible third party integrations (or for all functionalities of third party software). Rather, they are intended as examples and launching points for Customers to create their own Custom Mappings. 

KBA 3592344 explains this further. It also outlines that you can try to create/vote on requests in the SAP Customer Influence program to ask for more built-in templates (KBA 3391676). But all resources required to create custom Mappings for your third party software are already published and available to Customers. It is not a requirement to start with a built-in Mapping. The main two resources to create a custom Mapping are the third party software's API documentation, and the pages related to Mappings in the API Guide for SAP Cloud ALM. The information in those documents can be used to create a custom Mapping for your third party software integration. The API guide also includes many examples to explain the Mapping structure and how to write it for specific outcomes.

entry.title: Re: Bucket processing in Vistex
entry.content:
Calculation Buckets
Information to be stored in calculation buckets is based on configured calculation bucket types. Use
transaction code /IRM/IPCLBCM) to define a calculation bucket type for each application and source
document combination. Supported types of source documents are:
• Sales document
• Billing document
• Delivery
• Purchase document
• Claim
• Transaction document

A configured bucket group is assigned to the calculation bucket. The bucket group stores the calculation
procedure and condition search profile used to determine the agreement used to update the line item
buckets.

Use the Calculation Bucket Workbench (/IRM/IPCLBM) to create calculation buckets, which can capture
lean data from source documents related to an agreement and/or partner. The calculation bucket
includes only those fields specified during bucket definition. Bucket data is visible in the
transactional/composite calculation runs and tracking. Data can be stored or, for virtual calculation
buckets, pulled to create calculation runs for posting.
Buckets can be updated either:
• Automatically, from agreements or settlement parameter groups
• In batch, using the Mass Processing of Calculation Buckets (/IRM/IPCLBPMP) transaction
• Manually, from the Calculation Bucket Workbench (/IRM/IPCLBM)
Source Document Buckets
Claim/transaction document data can be stored in buckets created in the following workbenches:
Claim Bucket Workbench (/IRM/IPBCKCRM)
Buckets are populated directly from claims when the claims are created or maintained. If you
change the conditions of a bucket, you need to reconstruct that bucket to delete and recreate
the bucket items. Reconstruction can be run either from the workbench or by running
Reconstruct Claim Buckets (/IRM/IPBCK27).
Transaction Bucket Workbench (/IRM/IPBCKRCAM)
Buckets are populated directly from transaction documents when the transaction documents
are created or maintained. If you change the conditions of a bucket, you need to reconstruct
that bucket to delete and recreate the bucket items. Reconstruction can be run either from the
workbench or by running Reconstruct Transaction Buckets (/IRM/IPBCK28).

Composite Buckets                                                                                                                              Calculation buckets can be used as a source for mapping to subcomponent key figures. The calculation
bucket type is stored in the subcomponent definition.

Bucket Processing
The Mass Processing of Business Register Source Documents (/IRM/IPBRCLBSMP) transaction can be
used to process (build calculation buckets), reprocess (delete and recreate calculation buckets), and
reprice (to reprice line items).

Config-IRM/IPSPRO-ECC
Basic setting->pricing-sales->Pricing agreement->Maintain IP agreement type
Assign-Bucket type
            Creation bucket
            Bucket validity

Document->Sales-Item criteria sales document
Assign-Bucket profile to IP-Item category

BR,
Aakash

entry.title: what is the most efficient to ccnnect PI with ERP
entry.content:
Hello,

I have an on prem PI system is connected via REST to an ERP system

The PI system is using an kafka adaper to read lets say 5 million records. I can easily test this.

I am trying to make it run quicker by scaling up first. i.e i can add cpu and ram fairly easily.

The reading of the kafka topic is ok. About 1.5 hours but the calling of the REST service and the custom code on the other side takes so much cpu and time on both sides. I just did a test and it took 4 hours just to send that many requests.  I know as I loaded all the kafka messages first into PI/PO then stopped the kafka connection and let it run the REST adapter.

I known how to scale up the ICM and disp processes etc and just about to do so in the next few days in my test environment. And I know there is some parallism thing I am playing with to scale up the PI REST Adapter to ERP.

But then i had a thought..... is REST between SAP PO/PI and SAP ERP really the most efficient way. 

PI/PO is surely for non-sap systems my source Kafka is non PO ok but my sink is ERP! 🙂

Is there not some more native connection between the two that is more efficient. I am not a SAP person and for various reasons do not have full access to a Basis Person so sort of learning by myself with some great help.

This is an onprem  ERP R3 running on oracle as is the PI all running on either LPAR for the ERP or RHEL on vmware for the PI. I do not need PI/PO for anything else as the Kafka adapter part. 

Looking forward to some creative ideas to make this run much quicker with less hardward. Which I can do but.... 🙂

Cloud even the adapter in the cloud is not an option.

entry.title: Please any ABAP code for Code Inspector to check SORT before Binary Search, not limited to SELECT?
entry.content:
In Code Inspector, there is an SAP test "Search problematic statements for result of SELECT/OPEN CURSOR without ORDER BY" (CL_CI_TEST_NO_ORDER_BY) which is able to detect among other things that a READ TABLE ITAB BINARY SEARCH is missing a previous sorting of ITAB, e.g. here SORT is missing:

REPORT.
TYPES tt_scarr TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF scarr WITH EMPTY KEY.
DATA(gt_scarr) = VALUE tt_scarr( ).
SELECT * FROM scarr INTO TABLE @GT_scarr.
*SORT gt_scarr BY carrname.
PERFORM form USING gt_scarr.

FORM form USING it_scarr TYPE tt_scarr.
  READ TABLE it_scarr WITH KEY carrname = 'British Airways' 
       BINARY SEARCH INTO DATA(ls_scarr).
ENDFORM.

But I'd like to extend this check to standard internal tables passed by SAP as parameters of user exits like workflow exit function modules, user exit function modules, BAdI implementations, etc., e.g. workflow start condition check (old interface):

FUNCTION z_check_fb_start_cond_eval
    IMPORTING
      VALUE(OBJTYPE) LIKE SWETYPECOU-OBJTYPE
      VALUE(OBJKEY) LIKE SWEINSTCOU-OBJKEY
      VALUE(EVENT) LIKE SWETYPECOU-EVENT
      VALUE(RECTYPE) LIKE SWETYPECOU-RECTYPE
    TABLES
      EVENT_CONTAINER LIKE SWCONT
    EXCEPTIONS
      ERROR_EVENT_CONTAINER_EMPTY.
*SORT event_container BY value.
  READ TABLE event_container WITH KEY value = 'A' 
       BINARY SEARCH INTO DATA(ls_element).
ENDFUNCTION.

Has anyone created such a custom Code Inspector check and can provide the code?

Thank you very much!

Sandra

NB: for information, there's also the simpler SAP internal test "Search READ .. BINARY SEARCH for unsorted results of SELECT statements" (CL_CI_TEST_SELECT_BIN_SEARCH) which does the same as CL_CI_TEST_NO_ORDER_BY for the above-described case.

entry.title: Re: Schedule Update of Down Payment Status - Sales Orders
entry.content:
Hi there, I doubt something like this exists in on-premise S/4 Cloud. But I would say, try something like this in OP:

 

  1. Create sales order with billing document plan (FAZ - down payment request)
  2. down payment request integrated with FI-CA - The FAZ item creates a request document in FI-CA, assigned to a contract account for the business partner.
  3. Incoming Payment Processed in FI-CA - Payment is posted and clears the FI-CA open item.
  4. Trigger PF Process (SD_ORDER_INTEGRATION) - Process Framework is used to check payment status and update sales order.
  5. Status handling class CL_CRMS4_IU_CM_PFP_SD is used to determine whether the payment is sufficient.
  6. Based on the payment, update user status on the SD order (e.g. removing delivery/fact block..).

Use BAdI: IF_CRMS4_IU_CM_PFP_SD_BADI: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/021b182b0c47416c8fafed67ebfd78a9/410797ee20e94997813fa62e3d77551e.html

framework Class: CL_CRMS4_IU_CM_PFP_SD (or subclass)

based on payment status in FI-CA, you can conditionally remove billing block in VBAP.

 

Hope this at least gives you some new ideas/thoughts.

 

Best,

Raf

entry.title: Re: SAP MDG-S integration with 3rd party onboarding system
entry.content:
Dear user,

And thank you for asking your question on the SAP Community blog.

Here are the main ways you can integrate a 3rd-party system with SAP MDG (for Suppliers and Materials):

1. Web Services / SOA (Service-Oriented Architecture)

  • MDG provides standard Enterprise Services (ES) that are available via SAP SOA Manager.
  • You can consume MDG services such as:
    • BusinessPartnerSUITEBulkReplicateRequest_In (for suppliers)
    • MaterialERPBulkReplicateRequest_In (for materials)
    • BusinessPartnerSUITEBulkCreateRequest_In
  • These services allow create/update/replicate operations.
  • Good option for real-time or near-real-time integration.
  • Can be used by your third-party system to push onboarding data into MDG.

Tip: These services follow SAP's data model, so mapping may be needed between the third-party system’s data and MDG’s structures (like BP, LFM1, LFA1, etc.).

2. IDoc-based Integration

  • Use standard IDocs such as:
    • CREMAS (for supplier master)
    • MATMAS (for material master)
  • This is more batch-oriented but very stable and widely used in legacy scenarios.
  • Can be triggered from SAP PI/PO or even from a flat-file converted to IDoc format.

3. SAP PI/PO or SAP Integration Suite (Cloud Integration)

  • Acts as a middleware layer between the third-party system and MDG.
  • Enables message transformation, routing, and protocol mediation (e.g., HTTPS to SOAP).
  • Preferred if your 3rd-party onboarding tool doesn’t speak SOAP natively or needs additional logic/transformation.
  • You can build custom iFlows for inbound create/change requests or for validations before MDG processing.

4. File-based Import (Staging via Data Import Framework)

  • You can import data into MDG using:
    • MDG Data Import Framework (available in MDG Classic)
    • Staging tables → Data replication
  • This is good for bulk loads (e.g., periodic onboarding from third-party source).
  • You can use:
    • CSV files, Excel files
    • Or custom reports to process the input

5. Custom APIs (OData / REST-based)

  • In case your 3rd-party onboarding tool prefers modern APIs, you can expose:
    • Custom OData Services via Gateway
    • Or build REST adapters in SAP BTP/SAP API Management that wrap MDG's standard SOAP services
  • Best for more flexible integration and newer platforms that don't support SOAP.

6. SAP Master Data Integration (SAP MDI) / Master Data Hub (optional)

  • If you're already using or planning to use SAP MDI as a centralized governance tool across cloud/on-prem systems, it can integrate MDG and your third-party system via a hub approach.
  • However, this adds an additional layer and is relevant mainly in a multi-SAP landscape.

 

I hope this will help you with finding the right fit for your business needs.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: VBEP S/4 HANA VS R/3
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question on the SAP Community blog.

Let's walk through each of your questions step by step to clarify how VBEP has evolved in SAP S/4HANA compared to R/3, especially with regard to open quantities, new fields, and how it compares to VBBE :

 

1. New Fields in VBEP (S/4HANA)

The fields you mentioned — DLVQTY_BU, DLVQTY_SU, OCDQTY_BU, OCDQTY_SU, ORDQTY_BU, ORDQTY_SU — are part of the data model simplification and enhancement in S/4HANA.

These fields represent quantities in different units of measure (BU = Base Unit, SU = Sales Unit) and are meant to give more granular and real-time visibility into the schedule line status.

Here’s what each means:

Field

Description

DLVQTY_BU

Delivered quantity in Base Unit of Measure (BU)

DLVQTY_SU

Delivered quantity in Sales Unit (SU)

OCDQTY_BU

Open confirmed delivery quantity in Base Unit

OCDQTY_SU

Open confirmed delivery quantity in Sales Unit

ORDQTY_BU

Ordered quantity in Base Unit

ORDQTY_SU

Ordered quantity in Sales Unit

These are redundant fields introduced to improve performance and analytics, avoiding on-the-fly UoM conversions when doing reporting or CDS-based analytics.

These fields are filled in real-time via internal processing (e.g., ATP updates) and are not directly editable via standard transactions.

2. Can I use ODN_AMOUNT to get the open value per schedule line?

Yes — but with some caution.

  • ODN_AMOUNT (Open Delivery Net Amount) is calculated at the schedule line level, unlike some traditional values in VBAP or VBAK.
  • It provides the net amount value (in document currency) corresponding to the open (undelivered) quantity at the schedule line.
  • You can use this field if you want to track open revenue/value for a specific delivery date.

Attention : however, if your pricing includes surcharges, header discounts, or conditions not distributed at the schedule line level, the amount might not match 100% exactly with expectations unless conditions are properly distributed.

3. Is VBEP filtered by open quantity ≠ 0 equivalent to VBBE?

Not quite, although they overlap in use case.

Key differences:

Feature

VBEP with Open Qty ≠ 0

VBBE (Requirements Table)

Source

Schedule line table

ATP (MRP) elements

Includes

All schedule lines (even fulfilled ones if not filtered)

Only open requirements

Use case

Sales schedule details, value tracking

ATP, availability check, MRP planning

Scope

Document-centric

Stock/requirement-centric

So:

  • If you filter VBEP by open quantities (e.g., OCDQTY_BU ≠ 0), you're close to what you get in VBBE, but:
    • VBEP may show more granular info (multiple lines per item/date)
    • VBBE is compiled from VBEP, VBFA, VBKD and other sources during ATP/MRP processes

Best practice : Use VBEP when analyzing document-driven open quantities, and use VBBE for ATP/MRP logic and planning requirements.

 

I hope my explanation is clear enough and answer your main questions.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA Cloud Public without tax engine
entry.content:
Dear user,

And thank you for asking your question on the SAP Community blog.

In S4 S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition for Brazil, where tax complexity is extremely high and external tax engines (TTEs) are typically required to handle proper tax determination, calculation, and electronic invoicing (NF-e/NFS-e).

Let’s break this down with a clear and practical point of view, especially considering your customer’s unique scenario:

 

Is it technically possible to run S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition for Brazil without a Tax Engine?

Yes, it is technically possible, but only under very limited and manual conditions.

In your customer's case:

  • Low transaction volume
  • Manual service invoice issuance (via the Prefeitura SP website)
  • No need for automated tax determination or eDocument/NF-e integration
  • Intention to post manually in FI-AR (billing) and FI-AP/MM (invoices/receipts)

So yes, they could operate without a tax engine, but with major limitations and risks.

 

What would you lose or limit by avoiding the tax engine?

Area

What Happens Without a Tax Engine

Tax determination (ICMS/IPI/ISS)

Manual entry in every transaction

NFS-e/NF-e (electronic invoicing)

Not supported without TTE

Compliance with Brazilian tax rules

At risk, no validation against fiscal rules

Real-time integration with SEFAZ/Prefeitura

Unavailable

Fiscal reporting (SPED, GIA, etc.)

May require custom/manual workaround

Mass transaction processing

Not scalable

 

Use Case: Posting Only in FI

If the customer is:

  • Creating and sending service invoices outside SAP (in prefeitura system), and
  • Only posting financial entries manually in SAP (FI-AR and FI-AP), and
  • Not using MM/SD integrated flows for goods or services that require automated tax calculation,

 Then they can operate without a tax engine, because:

  • No automatic tax calculation is needed
  • No eDocument/NF-e generation is required
  • No external service integration is needed

But it's important to:

  • Get clear sign-off from tax and audit teams
  • Understand this setup will not scale well if business grows
  • Accept the risk of non-compliance if users mistype or miscalculate taxes

 

SAP’s Official Position (Best Practice)

SAP strongly recommends a tax engine for Brazil for any productive environment, because:

  • S/4HANA Cloud Public does not include native Brazilian tax logic
  • SAP does not deliver local tax content for Brazil (unlike some on-premise scenarios)
  • Tax engine is required for standard fiscal compliance

 

Recommendation :

For this specific low-volume, high-value service business, a "FI-only, manual entry" approach can work temporarily, as long as:

  • They have clear procedures and internal controls
  • They don’t expect SAP to calculate or determine taxes
  • They don’t require NF-e or NFS-e from within SAP
  • They are OK with not leveraging integrated SD/MM processes

But you should:

  1. Document this approach thoroughly.
  2. Include risks for audit/compliance.
  3. Suggest this only as a temporary or special-case solution.

 

I hope my explanation is clear enough and answer your main questions.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: Public Cloud and Sales&Service Cloud V2 - Difference...
entry.content:
Hi, 

Main filters

The role of main filters is to select objects for replication

Segment filters

You can choose the Show Segment Filters button to create or change filter criteria for segment filters. When you define a segment filter, you ensure that only selected segments of a business objects are replicated. Specifying segment filters reduces not the number of business objects that are replicated, but the size of each business object.

Best regards, Łukasz

entry.title: Can the internal job posting be restricted to some subset of the workfoce?
entry.content:
Some clients don't want to show to the entire employee population some specific job requisition but only to the employees that have specific jobs, or grad, or department, or location.
While posting in the internal career site, is there any why to give visibility to the Job Requisition to employees based on some rules or attributes\criteria?

Private posting is not a solution for 2 reasons:

1) if target employees forward the link to not target employees they can anyway see the job requisition

2) those internal job market campaigns often target thousands of employees, so recruiters should manage manually massive emails to share the private posting urls.

entry.title: The SAP Extended Warehouse Management FAQ Series: SCM Optimizer for EWM
entry.content:
With the introduction of Unified Package Building (UPB) in Extended Warehouse Management for SAP S/4HANA, many of our customers would like to understand more about how they could use the SCM Optimizer in their implementation projects and what are the aspects to consider.

We collected some interesting facts and typical questions on SCM Optimizer usage for EWM in the following blog post.

What is the SCM Optimizer?

The SCM Optimizer is a software (implemented in C/C++) that can be installed on the application server or on a separate service running on Windows or Linux. The SCM Optimizer, also called “Optimizer”, can be used by SAP backend solutions, such as Transportation Management or Extended Warehouse Management, for algorithms-based problem solving.

The software component SCM Optimizer contains a collection of different optimizer engines. Each engine is delivered as a separate executable inside the archive. The engines are specialized on different kinds of optimization scenarios, e.g. scheduling or network planning, pallet building and more.

Find more information about the SCM Optimizer via SAP Note 1640509 - FAQ about the setup of SCM Optimizer.

Where is the SCM Optimizer used in EWM for SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition (PCE)?

Since SAP EWM 9.5, the SCM Optimizer (VSO engine) can be used in EWM through the Unified Package Build, enabling the integration of Package Building (PB) for packing in inbound processes, repacking, outbound delivery processes, as well as posting changes and stock transfer. Learn more about Unified Package Building in EWM and Unified Package Building via SAP Help.

As we gradually introduced new features, you can find an good overview on which process is supported by UPB via SAP Note information 3076865 - Unified Package Building: Additional Application-Specific Information.

While using the Unified Package Building (UPB) is scope of basic functions, the usage of Package Building (PB) in EWM is scope of advanced functions.

When to use the Package Building Optimizer (PBO)?

The advantage of PBO is that it can also build optimized pallets/packages for different sized products considering their dimensions and the weight. You do not need any package instructions or packaging specification, it does a kind of 3D Tetris to build the pallets. There are simple (stacking) rules available, but you can also configure it in more detail. In addition, the results can be visualized in a 3D scene.

How and where to install the SCM Optimizer?

If you are an on-premise customer, you can install the SCM Optimizer on a separate instance (recommended) or on the application service of the consumer solution (e.g. EWM, TM). However, please review SAP Note 1640509 - FAQ about the setup of SCM Optimizer highlighting the advantages and considerations guiding you on this decision.

Please as well review the installation guide mentioned via SAP Note 1686826 - Installation help for installing SCM Optimizers.

If you are a Private Cloud customer, and you have not yet used SCM Optimizer in e.g. your TM PCE system, our Enterprise Cloud Services (ECS) team will install the optimizer on your cloud infrastructure. You can contact your local ECS team and create service requests.

Do I have to perform a “SCM Optimizer migration” when I am transforming from Business Suite to SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition (PCE)?

No, migration activities are not required. The SCM Optimizer is a state-les solution, that does not have an own database or configuration-layer. So, it just can be installed in a greenfield approach during transformation projects. Please ensure that you install the latest optimizer patch available on service marketplace.

As a background, the SCM Optimizer is called from the backend solution with a configuration that is defined in the application layer (e.g. EWM, TM, etc.). This means that there is no migration of SCM Optimizer relevant, when moving from Business Suite (e.g. SAP EWM 9.5) to EWM in SAP S/4HANA (PCE).

What are the commercial aspects to consider using SCM Optimizer in EWM for SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition (PCE)?

In general the usage of Package Building including Package Building Optimizer in EWM is considered advanced scope (included in the advanced license).

If you are a PCE customer, please note that the infrastructure for the SCM Optimizer is not yet included in the EWM subscription. You could consider adding an additional SAP Connection Package for SCM Optimizer hosting.

In case you already have a subscription for Transportation Management for SAP S/4HANA, etc., the included optimizer ca be used by EWM too.
